assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The item exposed to foreign currency risk is an intragroup dividend-related receivable that has been recognised.
The issue is whether a hedge relationship could be documented under IFRS 9 for that foreign currency exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context provides the default IFRS 9 accounting basis when a hedge relationship is not applied, with fair value changes on derivatives generally recognised in profit or loss and amortised cost items accounted for outside hedge accounting.
